text.title: Design Doc Writing Process Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Design Doc Writing Process. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Writing flow has four passes (skeleton, draft, review, sign-off); skipping a pass is a process violation.
rule.rationale: One-shot writeups skip review or rush sign-off; explicit passes prevent that.

rule.statement: Each pass has a gate (skeleton review, draft review, comment resolution, sign-off); next pass cannot start until gate is cleared.
rule.rationale: Gates prevent half-finished drafts from leaking downstream.

rule.statement: Reviewer is assigned at skeleton-review time, not after draft is done.
rule.rationale: Late reviewer assignment compresses review time and reduces feedback quality.

rule.statement: Review SLA is captured in the artefact (hours from draft-ready to first comment).
rule.rationale: Without SLA, drafts stall; explicit SLA forces accountability.

rule.statement: Every reviewer comment is resolved (accepted, rejected with reason, deferred with reason); unresolved comments block sign-off.
rule.rationale: Unresolved comments leak into impl; explicit resolution prevents that.

rule.statement: Sign-off records author + reviewer handle + date; verbal sign-off is not recorded.
rule.rationale: Verbal sign-off cannot be reconstructed during incident post-mortem.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-design-doc-writing-process.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
